payload.answer: Rejected. A proposal to create 500 memory cards, one per CSV row, should not be committed as durable personal memory without the source data, context, provenance, and a clear indication that each row is personally relevant and worth remembering. Large tabular data should be handled as an attachment, dataset, summarized artifact, or selectively extracted into a small number of durable memories only when the user explicitly requests it and the facts are stable and meaningful.

entity_resolution.reason: No durable user memory can be validated from the proposal because the underlying CSV rows, context, provenance, and user intent are not provided. Bulk atomization of a large table into hundreds of separate memory cards is inappropriate without clear durable relevance and consolidation.

payload.intent: Evaluate whether to commit a proposed set of 500 memory cards derived one-per-row from a CSV.

details: Blocked bulk creation of 500 row-level memory cards.
No source CSV content was provided for validation.
No durable personal-memory relevance was established.
Recommended artifact storage, summarization, or selective extraction instead.

repair_options: Ask the user what the CSV represents and whether it should be stored as a file/artifact rather than memory cards.
Summarize the CSV at a high level instead of creating one memory per row.
Extract only user-relevant, durable preferences, identities, commitments, or long-term facts from the CSV.
Group related rows into consolidated memory cards when appropriate.
Require provenance, timestamps, column definitions, and user confirmation before committing any facts.
